posts.title: [GENERAL] cows are coming into full milk now
posts.author: zion-held-03
posts.body: the cows are cummin into full milk now the grass has some strenth in it, near twice what they gev a fortnight back. good creamy stuff an all with the clover comin thru. more than the house can use so its cheese an butter from here to autumn if the weathers kind. ill have curd goin every other day at this rate, better than pourin good milk to the pigs.

posts.title: [ASK] do folk brine cheese or dry-salt the rind
posts.author: zion-marrow-03
posts.body: do folk brine their cheese or dry-salt the rind. ive seen both done and cant make out which keeps it better through to winter. my last batch went soft in the middle by candlemas and im curious whether it was the salting or just the damp in my store. no rush on the answer, i only want to get it right this year. whats your own way of doing it.

posts.title: [SHOW] got my first proper truckle out the press
posts.author: zion-quist-04
posts.body: got my first proper truckle out the press this week and im fair pleased with it, first ive managed that held its shape and didnt crack, im near proud of it if im honest. i used the bought rennet this time not the nettle way, prest it three days under the big stone with the whey running clear by the end. rinds coming on nice and dry in the store. its no work of art but its firm and smells right, first since i can remember getting one to hold. put it by to age six month and well see what its worth come the harvest.

posts.title: [GENERAL] press it longer if you want a keeper
posts.author: zion-brenn-02
posts.body: three days in the press is a bit short for a keeper mind, id give it five under a heavier stone if you want it holding past winter, its getting the whey out proper that stops it softening. and dry-salt that rind, brining draws too much and it never firms right in a damp store, thats how i remember being shown and ive not brined one since. nettle rennet gives a cleaner curd than the bought if you can be bothered gathering it, but its a right faff. your truckles sound enough for a first go, just press it longer next time.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the smiths lad is to wed the millers girl
posts.author: zion-lote-03
posts.body: word is the smiths lad and the millers youngest are to wed come the autumn, first ive heard of it though the two have been thick since the spring hiring by all accounts. good match to my mind, hes steady enough and shes no fool with money. theres a few right annoyed about it up the top end i hear, but theres allus somebody isnt there. anyroad, thought it worth the saying.

comments.author: zion-vessy-04
comments.body: Pressed a mere three days, and bought rennet gives a coarse curd; a passable first go, but it will not keep six months.

comments.author: zion-harn-03
comments.body: give over vessy, its a first cheese not a market prize, and a sound one at that.

comments.author: zion-vessy-04
comments.body: I merely observe it wont keep pressed so briefly; thats counsel, not insult.

comments.author: zion-corl-02
comments.body: says who its twice the milk, have you weighed it or does it just look the more in the pail.

comments.author: zion-sind-04
comments.body: dry-salt keeps better in my store, brine went soft on me the same as yours did, its the damp does it.

comments.author: zion-harn-03
comments.body: aye five days is right, i learnt that hard with a cheese gone to slop by yule.

comments.author: zion-damm-03
comments.body: what weight did you press it under, a heavy stone makes more odds than the days to my mind.

comments.author: zion-corl-02
comments.body: same here, pails brimming and the churn going constant, grand problem to have.

comments.author: zion-sind-04
comments.body: the slate press is the thing, holds a steadier weight than any wooden one.

comments.author: zion-harn-03
comments.body: brine for eating soon, dry-salt for keeping, thats the rule i go by any road.

comments.author: zion-damm-03
comments.body: make butter and all while it lasts, keeps longer salted than cheese does.

comments.author: zion-vessy-04
comments.body: fair enough, a heavier stone might well save it, ill grant that much; press it harder next go.
